cataract: Fundus image showing cataract, characterized by lens opacity that obscures the view of the retina.
Cataract appears as a hazy or blurred image with reduced clarity of retinal details.
The image may reveal diffuse cloudiness and decreased red reflex due to lens changes.
Cataracts are a common cause of visual impairment, especially in older adults.
This class is defined by the presence of lens opacification affecting retinal visualization.
diabetic_retinopathy: Fundus image showing diabetic retinopathy, characterized by microaneurysms, hemorrhages, and exudates.
Diabetic retinopathy appears as scattered red or yellow lesions, cotton wool spots, and neovascularization.
The image may reveal retinal edema, venous beading, and proliferative changes.
Diabetic retinopathy is a complication of diabetes and can lead to vision loss if untreated.
This class is defined by vascular abnormalities and retinal damage due to chronic hyperglycemia.
glaucoma: Fundus image showing glaucoma, characterized by increased cupping of the optic disc and thinning of the neuroretinal rim.
Glaucoma appears as an enlarged optic cup with a pale disc and loss of peripheral retinal nerve fibers.
The image may reveal notching of the rim and peripapillary atrophy.
Glaucoma is a progressive optic neuropathy that can cause irreversible vision loss.
This class is defined by structural changes in the optic nerve head and retinal nerve fiber layer.
normal_retina: Fundus image of a healthy retina, showing clear visualization of the optic disc, macula, and retinal vessels.
A normal retina appears with sharp disc margins, uniform color, and no evidence of pathology.
There are no signs of hemorrhages, exudates, or abnormal vascular changes in a normal retina image.
The image serves as a reference for comparison with pathological retinal findings.
Normal retina images reveal intact retinal structure and regular vascular patterns throughout.
